title: diplacrum pygmaeum
intro: Diplacrum pygmaeum is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as a small club-rush. It is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Diplacrum pygmaeum has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which have been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and relieving pain.
